steps.title: Find the number of gaps between clips
steps.content:
The wall spans from the first clip at the left edge to the last clip at the right edge, a distance of $4x$ feet. Each consecutive clip is $\frac{4}{7}$ feet apart. The number of gaps between clips is:
$$\text{Number of gaps} = \frac{4x}{\frac{4}{7}} = 4x \cdot \frac{7}{4} = 7x$$

steps.title: Apply the fence post principle to count clips
steps.content:
When a length is divided into equal gaps from one end to the other, the number of boundary points (clips) is always one more than the number of gaps. With $7x$ gaps, the total number of clips is:
$$y = 7x + 1$$
This matches the correct answer — D is correct.
